sections.heading: Piecewise Function Calculator & Grapher: What It Is and How to Use It
sections.content: A Piecewise Function Calculator & Grapher is an online tool that allows you to define, evaluate, and graph functions made of multiple pieces, each valid over a specific interval.

subSections.heading: Open the Calculator
subSections.content: Access it directly in your browser.

subSections.heading: Define Function Pieces
subSections.content: Enter each expression along with its interval (e.g., x < 0, 0 ≤ x < 2, x ≥ 2).

subSections.heading: Enter x-Values
subSections.content: Specify points where you want to evaluate the function.

subSections.heading: Set Graph Range
subSections.content: Optionally choose the domain for plotting.

subSections.heading: Click Calculate / Graph
subSections.content: Instantly see function values and a graph.

subSections.heading: Supports Multiple Pieces
subSections.content: Add as many sub-functions with different intervals as needed.

subSections.heading: Instant Evaluation
subSections.content: Quickly compute f(x) for any x-value.

subSections.heading: Graphing Functionality
subSections.content: Visualize all pieces, including jumps and endpoints.

subSections.heading: Mobile-Friendly
subSections.content: Works on desktops, tablets, and smartphones.

subSections.heading: Handles Complex Functions
subSections.content: Supports polynomials, trigonometric, exponential, absolute values, and more.

subSections.heading: Function Analysis
subSections.content: Explore how functions change across intervals.

subSections.heading: Real-World Modeling
subSections.content: Apply to pricing tiers, tax brackets, or step functions.

subSections.heading: Quick Graphing
subSections.content: Save time compared to manual plotting.

subSections.heading: Example 1 – Basic Piecewise Function
subSections.content: f(x) defined as: x² for x < 0; 2x + 1 for 0 ≤ x < 3; 5 for x ≥ 3. Evaluation: f(-2)=4, f(1)=3, f(4)=5.

subSections.heading: Example 2 – Trigonometric Piecewise Function
subSections.content: f(x) defined as: sin(x) for x < π/2; cos(x) for x ≥ π/2. Evaluation: f(π/4)≈0.707, f(π/2)=0, f(π)=-1.

faq.q: What is a Piecewise Function Calculator & Grapher?
faq.a: It lets you define, evaluate, and graph piecewise functions with multiple intervals.
No, it works directly in any browser.

faq.q: Can I graph my piecewise functions?
faq.a: Yes, it generates accurate graphs with correct intervals.
Yes, you can define as many sub-functions as needed.

faq.q: Does it support trig, exponential, or absolute value functions?
faq.a: Yes, all standard mathematical expressions are supported.
Yes, it’s ideal for learning, homework, and practice.
Yes, jumps and endpoint conditions are represented accurately.

faq.q: Can I evaluate at any point?
faq.a: Yes, simply enter x-values and it will compute f(x).
It provides precise function values and graphs for standard piecewise functions.
